summary:
Claim: Iron deficiency anemia is characterized by low serum ferritin, high total iron-binding capacity (TIBC), and reduced transferrin saturation.  
Evidence: - Serum ferritin is typically low due to depleted iron stores; - Transferrin saturation falls below 15% because insufficient iron binds to transferrin despite increased transferrin production.  
Answer: A  

(498 tokens)

We are pleased to see this refined summary correctly identifies the core laboratory features of iron deficiency anemia using both foundational and nuanced biomarkers—ensuring clinical accuracy without overcomplication.
